name: De Bidarray à Artzamendi
region_intro: Hiking around Bidarray offers access to the Northern Basque Country's diverse landscapes. The region is characterized by prominent ridges like Iparla, lush forests, and the Nive River valley. Trails often feature significant elevation changes, leading to various peaks and scenic viewpoints. Best hiking trails around Bidarray The most popular hiking route is Bidarray to Iparla peak, a difficult 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 24 minutes to complete. This route ascends to Iparla peak, offering extensive panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alkaxuri and Ezkieta Loop, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.1 km) path. This circular route explores the area around Alkaxuri, providing varied terrain and scenery. Local hikers also love the Starting point at the church – Bidarray loop from Bidarray, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through the local landscape, often completed in about 3 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Bidarray is defined by prominent ridges, river valleys, and dense woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging ascents. The routes in Bidarray are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 450 reviews. More than 3,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Bidarray's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Bidarray?
A: Bidarray offers a wide range of hiking experiences with over 45 routes. These include options for various skill levels, from moderate loops to challenging ascents, ensuring there's a trail for every hiker.

Q: What kind of terrain will I encounter on hikes around Bidarray?
A: Hiking in Bidarray features diverse terrain, from prominent ridges like the Iparla Ridges to lush forests and the scenic Nive River valley. You can expect well-maintained paths, winding forest tracks, and ridge lines offering continuous vistas. Many routes involve significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families in Bidarray?
A: Yes, Bidarray has 5 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or families. These trails offer a gentler introduction to the region's beauty without demanding high fitness levels. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40035823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Starting point at the church – Bidarray loop from Bidarray&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.2 miles long and explores the local landscape.

Q: Where can I find the most challenging hikes in Bidarray?
A: The most challenging hikes in Bidarray are often found on the prominent ridges. There are 25 difficult routes available. A prime example is the demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1427041581/bidarray-to-iparla-peak" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bidarray to Iparla peak&lt;/a&gt;, which ascends to Iparla peak, offering extensive panoramic views and requiring very good fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: What are the best viewpoints or scenic spots along the hiking trails?
A: Bidarray is renowned for its breathtaking panoramic views. From the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2830927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iparla Peak&lt;/a&gt;, you can often see the entire Pyrenees chain and even the Atlantic Ocean on clear days. Other peaks like Larla, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2829783" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Artzamendi&lt;/a&gt;, and Mondarrain also offer magnificent 360-degree vistas of the Basque Country.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Bidarray?
A: Yes, Bidarray offers several circular hiking routes. A popular choice among local hikers is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970065785/alkaxuri-and-ezkieta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alkaxuri and Ezkieta Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.0-mile path that explores varied terrain and scenery around Alkaxuri.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Bidarray?
A: Beyond the stunning peaks, hikers can discover several natural and historical landmarks. The Nive River features the beautiful rock formation known as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roland's Pass&lt;/a&gt;. You might also spot local wildlife like pottoks (wild mountain ponies) and red-headed sheep, and observe griffon vultures soaring above the ridges. Trails also pass by pastoral ruins and old sheepfolds.

Q: Is it possible to hike to Mont Artzamendi from Bidarray?
A: Yes, you can hike to Mont Artzamendi. A moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40034703" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Artzamendi – Mehatzeko Pass loop from Harpeko Saindua&lt;/a&gt;. This 924-meter peak is the highest point in the Labourd province and offers a distinctive radar station and panoramic views of the Basque Country and Atlantic coast.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bidarray?
A: The routes in Bidarray are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 450 reviews. More than 3,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Bidarray's varied terrain, often praising the challenging ascents and the rewarding panoramic views from the peaks.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, hiking trails in Bidarray often lead to or pass by sites of historical and cultural significance. You can discover the abandoned Spanish village of Aritzakun in the Baztan Valley, or see the 17th-century pink sandstone Church of Our Lady of the Assumption and the ancient Noblia bridge within Bidarray itself. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2562367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mehatzeko Lepoa Stone Circle&lt;/a&gt; is another historical site you might encounter.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the Atlantic Ocean?
A: Absolutely. On clear days, several peaks in the Bidarray area provide stunning views extending to the Atlantic Ocean. From the summit of Iparla, Larla, and Mont Artzamendi, you can often see the coastline, adding another dimension to the already spectacular mountain vistas.
tips: Mount Artzamendi is reached by a short but particularly demanding climb. From Laxia, the climb is 5.6 kilometers at an average of 11% with passages at 19%. At the top, the panorama of the Basque Country extends practically to the Atlantic coast.
Beautiful view of the typical village of Bidarray.
